title: SIGSEGV during graceful shutdown of Eshkol-compiled long-running binaries (teardown-ordering race, related to ESH-0119)
goal: Selene PRIORITY 3 (2026-07-06 night build note): during a resident redeploy, SIGTERM to the running compiled `noesis` resident logs the runtime's own graceful-shutdown message, then several `[Eshkol] fatal signal: SIGSEGV` lines at distinct addresses appear during/after teardown, interleaved with the next resident's startup logs. Steady-state is rock solid; only the SIGTERM/shutdown path is affected. Assess and fix the ordering race.
root_cause: Two compounding architectural gaps. (1) eshkol_runtime_shutdown() never stopped/joined the runtime's own worker-thread pool (lib/backend/thread_pool.cpp's global parallel-map/parallel-execute pool) before running arbitrary shutdown hooks and restoring signal handlers. A shutdown hook (the documented, encouraged way for a module to free its own arena-backed state on graceful shutdown, eshkol_register_shutdown_hook) that frees shared state races a pool worker still mid-task touching that same state -> use-after-free SIGSEGV, reported by the still-installed fatal-signal handler (eshkol_runtime_restore_signals() hadn't run yet), often from more than one racing worker hence 'distinct addresses'. (2) LLVM codegen emits eshkol_runtime_init() at the top of a compiled program's C main() but NEVER emits the paired eshkol_runtime_shutdown() before main() returns, so a standalone AOT-compiled resident (exactly noesis's shape) never goes through the runtime's own ordered teardown at all -- process exit relied solely on the unordered libc atexit/static-destructor chain, with no guarantee worker threads are stopped before other global state is torn down.
fix: (1) lib/core/runtime_lifecycle_hosted.cpp: eshkol_runtime_shutdown() now calls a new shutdown_all_thread_pools() (declared in runtime_hosted_internal.h) immediately after draining tracked in-flight operations and BEFORE run_shutdown_hooks()/eshkol_stop_timer()/eshkol_runtime_restore_signals(). It calls thread_pool_global_shutdown() (idempotent, safe even if the pool was never created) so no Eshkol worker thread can be running for the rest of shutdown or after it returns. (2) lib/backend/vm_parallel.c: exported eshkol_vm_parallel_shutdown_global (was static vm_parallel_shutdown_global) for potential future wiring from the interpreter/eval drivers -- deliberately NOT called from shutdown_all_thread_pools() itself, because doing so force-links the whole bytecode-VM unity object (and its GPU/Metal/ObjC dependencies) into every AOT-compiled Eshkol binary via eshkol-runtime, confirmed experimentally (a minimal program linking only libeshkol-runtime.a failed to link with undefined Metal/ObjC symbols once something referenced it). The VM's own parallel pool only backs `-r`/interpreted and eval-mode parallel-map, which already goes through drivers (exe/eshkol-run.cpp, exe/eshkol-repl.cpp) that fully link the VM and already call eshkol_runtime_shutdown() before returning -- closing that analogous race belongs there directly, tracked as a follow-up, not bundled into this always-linked TU. (3) lib/backend/llvm_codegen.cpp: added emitRuntimeShutdownBeforeMainReturn(), called at all 3 sites that emit eshkol_runtime_init() (top-level-expression main, user-defined main, and the defensive branch inside createMainWrapper), pairing every runtime_init with a runtime_shutdown(ESHKOL_SHUTDOWN_NONE) immediately before the corresponding CreateRet -- same REPL/WASM guard as the init call. eshkol_runtime_shutdown() is idempotent, so this is a no-op if a resident's own FFI-based SIGTERM handling already called it.
acceptance: adversarial repro (shutdown hook frees a shared thread-safe arena while the global thread pool is mid-task) SIGSEGVs reliably pre-fix, 50/50 cycles clean post-fix
real compiled Eshkol resident (parallel-map in a tick loop, FFI-polled interrupt flag, SIGTERM) survives 50 external SIGTERM restart cycles with zero '[Eshkol] fatal signal' lines

scripts/run_stack_overflow_diagnostic.sh: genuine crash diagnostics unaffected (JIT + AOT both still report '[Eshkol] fatal signal: SIGBUS ... ' with a nonzero exit for deliberate deep recursion, since the crash happens inside user code before the new shutdown call is ever reached)
scripts/run_codegen_tests.sh + scripts/run_examples_tests.sh: 100% pass (no codegen-emission regressions from the new main()-wrapper calls)

freestanding toolchain tests (freestanding_object_smoke_test, freestanding_lowlevel_object_test, freestanding_elf_link_smoke_test) still pass -- the new call is gated by the identical guard as the existing eshkol_runtime_init() emission, so freestanding output is unaffected
runtime_signal_handlers_test and runtime_shutdown_hooks_test unit tests still pass
campaign_payoff: Closes a production SIGSEGV-during-shutdown risk for every long-running Eshkol-compiled resident (torn-write risk at exactly the wrong moment during a redeploy); makes eshkol_runtime_shutdown() the single authoritative, race-free teardown entry point for both the interpreter and AOT-compiled binaries.
resolution: RESOLVED 2026-07-06 (branch fix/shutdown-teardown-race). eshkol_runtime_shutdown() now stops/joins the global thread pool before running shutdown hooks or restoring signal handlers (lib/core/runtime_lifecycle_hosted.cpp, lib/core/runtime_hosted_internal.h); AOT codegen now pairs eshkol_runtime_init() with eshkol_runtime_shutdown() before main() returns at all 3 emission sites (lib/backend/llvm_codegen.cpp); lib/backend/vm_parallel.c's pool-shutdown exported for future wiring without forcing VM/GPU linkage into AOT binaries. Verified: adversarial repro (shutdown hook frees shared arena while pool workers are mid-task) reliably SIGSEGVs pre-fix and is 50/50 clean post-fix via 3 independent harnesses (standalone C++ repro, new tests/core/runtime_shutdown_teardown_race_test.cpp x50, and a real compiled Eshkol resident using parallel-map + FFI-polled interrupt flag x50 external SIGTERM cycles); ESH-0119 stack-overflow diagnostic unaffected (scripts/run_stack_overflow_diagnostic.sh both lanes PASS); SICP 88/88; parallel suite 7/7; codegen + examples suites 100%; freestanding toolchain tests unaffected.
